title: United Kingdom Data Residency Baseline (UK GDPR + DPA 2018)
notes: Constrains placement to UK cloud regions and evidences the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018. Post-Brexit the UK GDPR retains GDPR article numbering. Evidence of technical/organizational controls, not a cryptographic data-path proof.

rules.name: Workloads pinned to a UK region
rules.description: Every workload declares node placement constrained to an allow-listed UK cloud region (London) via topology.kubernetes.io/region.
rules.rationale: Placement outside the UK can transfer personal data to a third country without an appropriate safeguard under the UK GDPR.

rules.name: No unrestricted or extra-territorial egress
rules.description: Services do not proxy to external endpoints (ExternalName) and NetworkPolicies do not permit unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) egress.
rules.rationale: Open egress can move personal data outside the UK without a transfer safeguard.

rules.name: Storage uses customer-managed keys
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims reference a customer-managed key (CMK) so the controller controls the keys.
rules.rationale: Customer-managed keys reduce provider access to plaintext and strengthen the sovereignty posture.

rules.name: Encryption at rest declared
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims declare encryption at rest via the configured label/annotation.
rules.rationale: Encryption at rest is a baseline technical measure under the security-of-processing obligation.
